_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MCh/DNB/Equivalent in (Neurosurgery, if required Vascular Surgeon)
clinical_indications: Arteriovenous malformation (AVM) is a congenital vessel abnormality with abnormal communication between artery and vein
An AVM can occur anywhere in the body, but brain and spinal AVMs present substantial risks when they bleed

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment available?
Clinical presentation, history and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment available?
Documentation of indication for GKRS/SRS treatment (if applicable)?
Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge available?

common_queries: Clinical notes missing, incomplete, or not detailing history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure, or planned line of management.
Diagnosis not confirmed by imaging (CT/MRI Brain/Spine, Angiography).
Clinical presentation, history, or imaging not indicative of surgery.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) or operative notes missing/incomplete.
Discharge summary missing or lacking follow-up advice.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Care at Tertiary Hospital. SRS/GKRS procedure in hospitals with availability
clinical_key_pointers: Arteriovenous malformation (AVM) is a congenital vessel abnormality with abnormal communication between artery and vein
Nidus is a tangle of arteries and veins connected by at least one fistula.
An AVM can occur anywhere in the body, but brain and spinal AVMs present substantial risks when they bleed
surgical_options: Surgery - Surgical removal is a primary treatment for scalp AVMs when indicated.
